metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and specify the 'distinct throwaway identity' used in smoke-check step 3 — provide the Gerrit account name (e.g. `rebar-test-uploader`), the env-var that holds its credential (e.g. `TEST_UPLOADER_GERRIT_TOKEN`), and a one-line note on where/how it is provisioned (manual pre-condition, separate SSM param, or created inline by the script).
findings.finding: The smoke-check step 3 references 'a distinct throwaway identity' to push the synthetic change, but nowhere in the plan is that identity named, its credential sourced, or instructions given for creating it. An executor cannot implement step 3 — or make step 4's uploader-preservation assertion non-vacuous — without knowing what this identity is.
findings.location: AC5 (Tests), smoke-check-autolander.sh step 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a named test account (e.g. `rebar-test-uploader`) with its token at `/rebar/prod/test-uploader-gerrit-token` (same SSM prefix; fetched as `TEST_UPLOADER_GERRIT_TOKEN`). Step 3 pushes using `TEST_UPLOADER_GERRIT_TOKEN`; step 4 asserts the returned `uploader.username` equals `rebar-test-uploader`, not `RebarBotNava`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the 'distinct throwaway identity' used in AC3 step 3 (e.g., a dedicated `smoke-test-uploader` Gerrit account), specify where its credential is sourced (SSM param, admin-provisioned, or existing account), and add any required provisioning step to the Files or AC.
findings.finding: The smoke-check AC (step 3) requires pushing a synthetic change as 'a distinct throwaway identity' so 'uploader preserved is observable', but the plan nowhere names that identity, specifies its credential source, or describes how it is provisioned — making smoke-check-autolander.sh unimplementable as written for step 3 and the uploader-preservation assertion in step 4.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 3: 'push a synthetic change to refs/for/scratch-autolander (uploaded as a distinct throwaway identity so "uploader preserved" is observable)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'The throwaway uploader is the existing `gerrit-admin` account (or a dedicated `smoke-uploader` service account); its HTTP token is passed as `$UPLOADER_CRED` — an env var the operator sets before running the script (or read from `/rebar/prod/gerrit-admin-password` via SSM for the admin account). The script verifies `uploader.username == <that account name>`.' This keeps the script self-contained and the assertion meaningful.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Signed-off-by trailer to the synthetic commit in smoke-check-autolander.sh step 3 (e.g. `git commit --trailer 'Signed-off-by: Autolander Smoke Test <autolander-smoke@navateam.com>'`) to satisfy the rebar project's `requireSignedOffBy = true` constraint.
findings.finding: The AC3 smoke test step 3 pushes a synthetic commit to refs/for/scratch-autolander, but the rebar project.config enforces `requireSignedOffBy = true` globally on all refs/for/* pushes. A synthetic commit without a Signed-off-by trailer will be rejected by Gerrit at the receive-pack stage, causing the smoke test to fail at step 3 before it can test the rebase right at all.
findings.location: AC — Tests (AC3), step 3; infra/gerrit/project.config [receive] requireSignedOffBy = true
findings.suggested_fix: In smoke-check-autolander.sh step 3, when constructing the synthetic commit, append `--trailer 'Signed-off-by: Autolander Smoke <smoke@navateam.com>'` to the `git commit` invocation, matching the pattern in feature-branch-e2e.sh line 272.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-submit step to smoke-check-autolander.sh (between steps 4 and 5) that casts LLM-Review=+1 and Verified=+1 on the synthetic change using an admin or Service User credential (or document that the test uses a scratch branch exempt from submit-requirements via an `applicableIf` override), so the submit call at step 5 is not blocked by the gate.
findings.finding: The AC3 smoke test step 5 calls for submitting the synthetic change into scratch-autolander, but the rebar project's active submit-requirements (LLM-Review=MAX AND Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved) apply to all refs/heads/* — including the scratch-autolander branch. Without explicit bot votes casting LLM-Review=+1 and Verified=+1 on the synthetic change beforehand, the submit call will be rejected with a 409 (submit requirement not satisfied), not a 403. The plan does not specify how to satisfy these gate labels for the throwaway change.
findings.location: AC — Tests (AC3), step 5; infra/gerrit/project.config [submit-requirement 'LLM-Review'] and [submit-requirement 'Verified']
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) before the step-5 submit call, use the admin credential to POST to `/a/changes/{id}/revisions/current/review` with `{"labels":{"LLM-Review":1,"Verified":1}}` to satisfy both requirements; or (b) add a per-branch submit-requirement override in project.config scoped to `refs/heads/scratch-autolander` with `applicableIf = is:false` so the smoke branch bypasses the gate entirely — then document that override clearly as test infrastructure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and provision the 'throwaway identity' used for the step 3 push in `smoke-check-autolander.sh`: specify the Gerrit username, email, credential source (SSM param or admin-created HTTP password), and any git config values needed to make that identity the recorded uploader.
findings.finding: The smoke-check step 3 protocol references 'a distinct throwaway identity' as the push actor, but this identity (Gerrit username, email, HTTP token or SSH key, and how/where it is provisioned) is never defined anywhere in the plan. An executor cannot implement the uploader-preservation assertion without knowing what identity to use.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 3: 'push a synthetic change to refs/for/scratch-autolander (uploaded as a distinct throwaway identity so "uploader preserved" is observable)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated Gerrit account (e.g., `smoke-uploader`) with an HTTP password stored in SSM at `/rebar/prod/smoke-uploader-token`; specify `git config user.name 'Smoke Uploader'` and `user.email smoke-uploader@navateam.com` in the clone used for the push. The AC assertion then checks `.uploader.username == 'smoke-uploader'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add Signed-off-by trailer handling to the smoke-check-autolander.sh spec for the step 3 synthetic commit push (e.g., install a prepare-commit-msg hook or pass `--trailer 'Signed-off-by: ...'` to `git commit`), matching the pattern in `feature-branch-e2e.sh` lines 263-275.
findings.finding: The smoke-check step 3 pushes a synthetic commit to `refs/for/scratch-autolander`, but the live `project.config` enforces `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` on all `refs/for/*` pushes. The plan specifies no Signed-off-by trailer handling for the synthetic commit, so the push will be rejected by Gerrit before the rebase step (step 4) is reached.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 3; infra/gerrit/project.config line 40 ([receive] requireSignedOffBy = true)
findings.suggested_fix: In the smoke-check-autolander.sh spec, after cloning the repo for the step 3 push, add: install the Gerrit commit-msg hook (`curl .../tools/hooks/commit-msg`), set `git config user.email` and `user.name`, and use `git commit -s -m 'smoke: autolander test'` (the `-s` flag appends Signed-off-by automatically). This mirrors the pattern in feature-branch-e2e.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the 'distinct throwaway identity' for smoke-test step 3 is provisioned — either name a pre-existing second Gerrit test account and where its credential comes from, or add a provisioning step (e.g. a dedicated `smoke-uploader` service account created by the smoke test's setup phase).
findings.finding: The smoke test step 3 requires pushing a synthetic change 'as a distinct throwaway identity so uploader preserved is observable,' but no second Gerrit account for that throwaway identity is provisioned or referenced anywhere in this plan or its dependencies. Without a pre-existing second account, the bot and the uploader are the same identity, making the 'uploader ≠ RebarBotNava' assertion in step 4 impossible to satisfy, and the test would pass vacuously or fail with a push-auth error.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests (AC3), step 3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the Contributors membership pre-condition in AC2, e.g. `curl -s -u "$CRED" "$GERRIT_URL/a/groups/Contributors/members" | jq -e '[.[].username] | index("RebarBotNava") != null'`.
findings.finding: AC2 says 'verify `RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors`' but provides no concrete proving command. Without a command, an executor has no in-session way to confirm the pre-condition before concluding no submit ACL change is needed.
findings.location: AC2 (Submit is already covered)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to smoke-check step 2 (or as a pre-flight in setup-project.sh): `curl -sf -u "$AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN" "$GERRIT_URL/a/groups/Contributors/members" | jq -e '[.[].username] | index("RebarBotNava") != null' || { echo 'ERROR: RebarBotNava not in Contributors'; exit 1; }`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit smoke-test step (before step 3/push) that calls `GET /a/groups/Contributors/members` and asserts `RebarBotNava` is present, failing fast if absent, so the 'no submit grant needed' scope exclusion is verified at runtime rather than assumed.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 states 'verify RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors (so POST /submit does not 403)' but the smoke-test steps in AC5 (the concrete test script) do not include a step that explicitly verifies this membership. The membership is established via setup-project.sh's default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS="${CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS:-RebarBotNava}"`, which is additive-only and only fires when setup-project.sh is run. If `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` was overridden or setup-project.sh was last run before this default was set, the live group membership may not include `RebarBotNava`, but nothing in the smoke test checks `GET /a/groups/Contributors/members` to confirm it.
findings.location: AC2 ('verify RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors'); AC5 (smoke-check-autolander.sh steps 1-6)
findings.suggested_fix: Insert as step 2b in the smoke-check script: `curl -sf -u "$ADMIN_CRED" "$GERRIT_URL/a/groups/Contributors/members" | jq -e '[.[].username] | index("RebarBotNava") != null' || { echo 'RebarBotNava not in Contributors — run setup-project.sh'; exit 1; }`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact REST endpoint and request body for the rebase-on-behalf call in the smoke test spec: `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` with JSON `{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}`, and assert HTTP 200 before proceeding to the uploader-preserved check.
findings.finding: The smoke test (AC-Tests step 4) says 'rebase-on-behalf via the REST API' but never names the specific endpoint or its request body. The Gerrit REST endpoint is `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` with JSON body `{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}`, a new endpoint surface not previously exercised in this codebase. Without naming it, an implementer may call `POST /a/changes/{id}/submit` (the only previously-named submit endpoint) or the wrong path, and the smoke test spec cannot be verified as correct.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests (AC3), step 4
findings.suggested_fix: In AC-Tests step 4, replace 'rebase-on-behalf via the REST API' with: `curl -s -X POST -u "$BOT_CRED" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"on_behalf_of_uploader":true}' "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/changes/${CHANGE_ID}/rebase"` and assert HTTP 200; then proceed to the uploader assertion.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan to remove 'All-Projects capability' (e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + per-project Rebase ACL + submit ACL provisioning') so the title matches the body's explicit rejection of an All-Projects grant.
findings.finding: The plan's title says 'All-Projects capability' but the body explicitly and repeatedly rejects any All-Projects grant ('NOT All-Projects'). An executor reading only the title or a task-tracker summary could create an All-Projects ACL entry, which the plan itself identifies as wrong.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Files section / AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + rebar-project Rebase ACL + submit verification'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan to match what the body actually does, e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase-on-behalf ACL provisioning (rebar project)' — removing 'All-Projects capability' and 'submit ACL provisioning' from the headline since both are explicitly out of scope per the body.
findings.finding: The plan title promises 'All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' but the body explicitly does neither: it rejects putting anything in All-Projects (Rebase goes into the rebar project's own refs/meta/config) and explicitly skips submit ACL provisioning (Submit is already covered via Contributors membership). An implementer reading only the title would build the wrong thing.
findings.location: Plan title: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase-on-behalf access right (rebar project)' — this matches the three actual deliverables: SSM SecureString credential, fetch-secrets mapping, and rebar-project Rebase ACL grant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define `$CRED` explicitly in the smoke-check-autolander.sh spec: specify the variable assignment (e.g., `CRED="RebarBotNava:${AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN}"`) and confirm the Gerrit HTTP username that corresponds to the `autolander-gerrit-token` SSM credential.
findings.finding: The plan's concrete curl assertion command uses the variable `$CRED` (e.g., `curl -s -u "$CRED" ".../a/changes/{id}/detail..."`) but nowhere in the plan is `$CRED` defined — its format (user:token? token only?), assembly location, or sourcing from `$AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` / `.env` is unspecified.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 4: 'curl -s -u "$CRED" ".../a/changes/{id}/detail?o=CURRENT_REVISION" | jq -r ...'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the bare `$CRED` placeholder in the plan with the explicit pattern used in smoke-check.sh: `-u "${AUTOLANDER_USER}:${TOKEN}"` where `AUTOLANDER_USER` is a named env-var (e.g., defaulting to `RebarBotNava`) and `TOKEN` is read from the `.env` file or SSM.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (and document in the plan) that no boot-time or systemd-level consumer reads `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` from `/etc/rebar/.env`; if the autolander service is ever started before `fetch-secrets.sh` runs (i.e., before compose-up), add the param to `user_data.sh`'s PARAMS map and remove the 'read-only' claim.
findings.finding: The plan asserts user_data.sh is read-only/unchanged, but user_data.sh maintains its own independent SSM→env-var PARAMS map (lines 88-102) that is separate from fetch-secrets.sh. Every other token consumed by containers at runtime (gerrit-bot-token) appears in BOTH user_data.sh and fetch-secrets.sh. The plan does not explain why autolander-gerrit-token is exclusively routed through fetch-secrets.sh but not user_data.sh, leaving an unbacked asymmetry: if any boot-time path or systemd unit reads /etc/rebar/.env expecting AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN, it will be absent.
findings.location: Files section ('user_data.sh — read-only/unchanged'); AC1 ('fetched by fetch-secrets.sh ... into the shared .env as AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note in the plan (Files section or AC Credential) that `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` is intentionally NOT updated — `autolander-gerrit-token` is a compose-boot-only secret (like `github-oauth-client-id` and `reviewbot-tickets-pat`), following the established pattern.
findings.finding: The plan's Files section omits `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` from the edit set, but the ssm.tf comment in line 16 says 'EXACT secret parameter names — keep in sync with user_data.sh and ADR 0012'. A future implementer following that comment literally would add `autolander-gerrit-token` to user_data.sh's PARAMS map, causing boot-time failure (CHANGEME sentinel check) until the SSM slot is populated. The plan should explicitly call out that user_data.sh is intentionally NOT updated (following the established pattern for compose-only params).
findings.location: Files section (absence of user_data.sh); infra/terraform/ssm.tf line 16 comment; infra/terraform/user_data.sh PARAMS map
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ssm.tf bullet in Files: '`user_data.sh` is NOT updated — this token is compose-only (not needed at cloud-init time), following the same pattern as `github-oauth-client-id` and `reviewbot-tickets-pat`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `trap cleanup EXIT` (or equivalent cleanup-on-failure handler) to `smoke-check-autolander.sh` so that a mid-script failure (including the 403 fallback path) still resets/prunes the scratch branch and abandons any open synthetic change.
findings.finding: The smoke-check-autolander.sh script specifies cleanup in step 6, but no trap/cleanup-on-failure handler is defined for mid-script aborts (e.g., failure after step 3 pushes the synthetic change but before step 6 runs). This leaves open synthetic changes accumulating on scratch-autolander across repeated failed runs.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh — AC Tests (AC3), steps 3–6
findings.suggested_fix: At the top of the script define a `cleanup()` function that abandons any open change by ID (captured after the push in step 3) and resets/prunes the scratch branch, then register it with `trap cleanup EXIT`. This ensures cleanup runs on both success and failure paths.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the RETIRE path for `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token`: specify that decommissioning requires (a) revoking the Gerrit HTTP token in the Gerrit UI, (b) removing the key from the `local.rebar_secret_params` set and applying Terraform to delete the SSM parameter, and (c) removing `RebarBotNava` from `Contributors` (or the `rebase` grant) in `setup-project.sh`.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for the SSM SecureString `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token`. If the autolander bot is decommissioned, an active Gerrit HTTP token persists in SSM indefinitely without revocation guidance, and the `for_each` entry keeps it managed by Terraform (which won't delete it without an explicit set removal).
findings.location: infra/terraform/ssm.tf — AC Credential bullet; Files section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified `rebase` ACL token assertion: cite the specific Gerrit 3.14.1 `access-control.html` URL (e.g., `https://<host>/Documentation/access-control.html#category_rebase`) or equivalent versioned doc confirming that the project.config token `rebase` in `[access "refs/heads/*"]` is the right grant for the `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` REST endpoint with `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader=true`, and clarify whether 'submitters can always rebase' is a documented invariant or an assumption.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the `rebase` project.config [access] token is the correct Gerrit 3.14.1 mechanism for gating the REST rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader endpoint, and that 'submitters can always rebase per the ACL docs' — both cited only to 'this instance's access-control.html', which is not present in the repo, has no URL, and returns no search hits. This is a load-bearing unverified capability assertion: if the `rebase` ACL token is not the right key (e.g., the REST endpoint checks a different permission, or the token name changed between Gerrit versions), the entire ACL grant is silently ineffective, and the AC3 smoke test becomes the only safety net.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete) — 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pinned reference: e.g., 'Verified against Gerrit 3.14.1 docs: https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/+/refs/heads/stable-3.14/access-control.txt#rebase (token `rebase`, category ID `rebase`, applies to REST rebase endpoint).' If the instance's own docs page is the source, include the full URL in a comment in project.config or setup-project.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document the exact `rebase` ACL token spelling for this Gerrit 3.14.1 instance by consulting `access-control.html` on the live instance and recording the confirmed token name in the plan (or in a comment in project.config), so the implementation does not rely on an out-of-repo reference.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the `rebase` ACL token is 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' but that file is not in the repository and cannot be probed from the plan text. The `rebase` permission token is a real Gerrit 3.x ACL token, but its exact spelling in project.config (lower-case `rebase`) and its availability on this specific Gerrit 3.14.1 instance are Unverified from the plan. A token name mismatch (e.g. if the instance uses a different spelling or the permission is not listed) would cause `setup-project.sh` to silently accept the push while Gerrit ignores the unrecognised key.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a comment in the plan (and eventually in project.config) like: `# Verified: Gerrit 3.14.1 access-control.html lists 'rebase' as a valid [access] permission token; confirmed YYYY-MM-DD on rebar.solutions.navateam.com.`

findings.finding: The plan's central deliverable — `[access "refs/heads/*"] rebase = group Contributors` in project.config enabling REST rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader — is an unverified capability assertion. The plan cites 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' but no such confirmation artifact, URL, or captured doc exists in the repo. No `rebase =` line appears anywhere in infra/gerrit/project.config or setup-project.sh. Moreover, the parent ticket's file_impact still references the OLD mechanism ('All-Projects [capability] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader + submit on refs/heads/*'), indicating a recent design pivot from the global-capability to per-ref-ACL approach whose correctness on Gerrit 3.14.1 is unconfirmed. Gerrit's access-control model is version-specific; the exact config-file token name and its binding to the REST API's `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` field should be verified against the live instance's documentation before the plan is committed.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete)' bullet; Files: infra/gerrit/project.config, infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Run `curl -u admin:<pw> https://<host>/a/access/?project=All-Projects` and `?project=rebar` on the live instance, confirm `rebase` appears as a permission type in the response (alongside its label and REST binding), capture the output as a comment in setup-project.sh, then update the parent ticket file_impact to reflect the per-ref ACL approach.

findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit 'Rebase' access right on this instance (Gerrit 3.14.1) enables rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader via the REST API — citing 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' — but then immediately defers the exact access-right name and project.config stanza to implementation-time verification. No captured doc artifact, URL, or concrete project.config snippet is present in the plan or repo. This is an unresolved capability assertion: the central deliverable cannot be authored without first resolving what the plan explicitly leaves open.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet; Files: infra/gerrit/project.config + infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix:
Consult `https://<gerrit-host>/Documentation/access-control.html` on the live 3.14.1 instance, confirm the exact permission token name (likely `rebase` in lowercase, not `label-Rebase`), then add the verified snippet to the plan body under the Rebase AC:
```
[access "refs/heads/*"]
  rebase = group Contributors   # or a dedicated bot group
```
and note: 'Verified against Gerrit 3.14.1 access-control.html on <date>'. This eliminates the implementation-time ambiguity and ensures the project.config push succeeds with the right semantics.

findings.finding: The `Rebase` access-right config-file token for project.config is asserted to exist on this Gerrit 3.14.x instance but is unverified — no `rebase =` line appears anywhere in infra/gerrit/project.config or setup-project.sh, and the plan itself defers confirmation to implementation time ('Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form … against this instance's access-control.html'). If the ACL token differs from what the plan expects (e.g., it requires a capability-level grant or a plugin-specific name), the project.config push will either be silently ignored or rejected, leaving the bot unable to rebase on behalf of the uploader.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' + Files: infra/gerrit/project.config
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit pre-implementation step: `curl -u admin:<token> https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/a/access/rebar | python3 -m json.tool | grep -i rebase` — if the key appears in the response, record it; if absent, probe All-Projects. Encode the confirmed token in project.config and setup-project.sh before merging the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete idempotency proving command to AC5, e.g. run `setup-project.sh` twice and assert `git diff` on the fetched `refs/meta/config` is empty after the second run.
findings.finding: The idempotency claim for `setup-project.sh` ('re-run is a no-op') in AC5 has no stated proving command. An executor has no in-session way to verify this property holds after adding the new `rebase` stanza.
findings.location: AC5 (Tests), first bullet
findings.suggested_fix: After the main setup-project.sh invocation in the smoke-check (or as a separate CI step): `bash setup-project.sh && bash setup-project.sh && git -C "$REPO" diff HEAD -- project.config | diff /dev/null - && echo 'idempotent OK'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the AC numbering contradiction: rename the 5th bullet from 'Tests (AC3)' to 'Tests (AC5 / covers AC3)' and update AC4's reference from 'the AC3 smoke test' to 'the AC5 smoke test' (or 'the smoke test in the Tests AC').
findings.finding: AC4 references 'the AC3 smoke test is the checkpoint' but the smoke test content lives in the 5th bullet (labeled 'Tests (AC3)'), not in AC3 ('Rebase-on-behalf access right'). An implementer reading AC4 and looking for the smoke test in AC3 finds only an ACL stanza, not a test script. The parenthetical '(AC3)' in the 5th heading further implies the 5th bullet IS AC3, but it is actually the 5th criterion — creating a cross-section numbering contradiction between AC4 and the 5th AC's title.
findings.location: AC4 ('Fallback protocol') referencing 'AC3 smoke test'; AC5 heading ('Tests (AC3)')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4, change 'the AC3 smoke test is the checkpoint' → 'the AC5 smoke test is the checkpoint'. In the 5th bullet heading, change 'Tests (AC3)' → 'Tests (AC5 — verifies AC3)' to make clear the numbering and what is being tested.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md note capturing the decision to use the shared `Contributors` group for `RebarBotNava` (rather than a dedicated bot group), with the rationale and the constraint that any future policy change to `Contributors` must account for the autolander bot membership.
findings.finding: The plan places the automation identity `RebarBotNava` inside the `Contributors` group (a human-contributor group) to inherit Submit and Rebase rights, but this architectural decision — sharing a single ACL group between human contributors and a bot — is not captured in any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc. Future maintainers tightening `Contributors` policies (e.g., adding a mandatory code-review count) may break the autolander without realising a bot is in the group.
findings.location: AC — 'Submit is already covered' bullet; Files section (infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh)

findings.finding: AC5's smoke test says 'asserting the uploader is preserved' but provides no concrete structured command — it is a prose assertion with no jq/curl command against the Gerrit REST API, making the assertion unverifiable in-session (taxonomy item 6: prose-vs-structured assertion target).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 ('Tests')

findings.finding: AC3 describes a new multi-step integration smoke test (create synthetic change on scratch ref, call rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader REST API, assert uploader is preserved, submit, then abandon/clean the scratch change) but no file implementing this test is enumerated in the Files section. The existing `infra/gerrit/smoke-check.sh` covers only the review-bot; the autolander smoke steps are distinct and require a new or extended script.
findings.location: Files section / AC3 (Tests)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Files: `infra/gerrit/autolander-smoke-check.sh` — CREATE: bash script that (1) reads AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN from SSM, (2) pushes a throwaway commit to refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander as a test uploader, (3) calls POST /changes/{id}/rebase with on_behalf_of_uploader=true as RebarBotNava, (4) asserts the uploader account is unchanged, (5) calls POST /changes/{id}/submit, (6) abandons or deletes the scratch ref. Exit non-zero on any step failure.

findings.finding: The AC5 smoke test says the bot 'submits' the throwaway change and then 'abandons/cleans up the scratch change' — but a MERGED (submitted) change cannot be abandoned in Gerrit. This is a self-contradicting cleanup sequence. Additionally, the smoke test targets 'refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander' but does not specify creating the `scratch-autolander` branch first; without it, the push will be rejected by Gerrit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests (AC3), last bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC5 test flow to: (1) `gerrit create-branch rebar scratch-autolander main` (idempotent); (2) push the synthetic commit to `refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander`; (3) bot rebases-on-behalf and asserts uploader is preserved; (4) **abandon** (do NOT submit) the change via `POST /a/changes/<n>/abandon`; (5) optionally delete the scratch branch. This keeps cleanup simple and avoids the merged-change abandonment impossibility. If submit must be proven, replace step 4 with submit + `DELETE /projects/rebar/branches/scratch-autolander` to clean the branch.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
